repo_signals: L3 smoke for code.database.cassandra: query-first partition key design, clustering columns, tombstones and compaction, tunable consistency QUORUM, denormalization, wide-row hotspots, LWT cost

retrieval_query: Cassandra partition key clustering column tombstone compaction consistency level quorum denormalization wide row LWT

validation_smoke.name: code.database.cassandra L3 validation smoke
signals: nodetool tablehistograms shows partition sizes bounded well under the warning threshold
a read at LOCAL_QUORUM returns consistent data after a node restart with the write at LOCAL_QUORUM
the query runs without ALLOW FILTERING because the partition key is fully specified
tracing shows the read touched a small, bounded number of SSTables rather than scanning many

diagnosis_smoke.name: code.database.cassandra diagnosis smoke
signals: nodetool reports a partition far above the 100MB warning size, signalling an unbounded partition
read latency spikes and tracing shows thousands of tombstones scanned before returning live rows
a coordinator hotspot appears because a low-cardinality partition key concentrates traffic on few nodes
a query requires ALLOW FILTERING, meaning it scans partitions instead of seeking by key
frequent LWT (IF NOT EXISTS) operations serialize through Paxos and collapse write throughput
